questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An Otto cycle has compression ratio 6, γ = 1.3. Efficiency (%) is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Wahl factor K_w:
options: Corrects for curvature and shear. K_w = (4C-1)/(4C-4) + 0.615/C
questions.solution: Wahl factor: K_w > 1. τ_max = K_w·(8FD)/(πd³). Closely related to Bergsträsser factor.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Specific fuel consumption (SFC):
options: Fuel consumed per unit power output (kg/kWh)
questions.solution: BSFC = ṁ_f/BP (kg/kWh). Lower BSFC = more efficient. Typical diesel BSFC ≈ 200-250 g/kWh.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The second law of thermodynamics implies that heat, on its own, flows from:
options: A hotter body to a colder body
A colder body to a hotter body
questions.solution: Spontaneous heat flow is from high to low temperature; the reverse needs work (Clausius statement).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a lathe, the principal cutting operation produces a surface of revolution by:
questions.solution: Turning removes material from a rotating workpiece using a single-point tool.

questions.solution: Rule of mixtures: upper bound for stiffness.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge

questions.solution: σ = 5.67×10⁻⁸ W/m²K⁴. ε = emissivity (0-1). Blackbody: ε = 1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Superheating in the evaporator:
options: Ensures only vapor enters the compressor (protects from liquid slugging)
questions.solution: Superheat: vapor heated above saturation before leaving evaporator. Prevents wet compression.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Carnot cycle:
options: Two isothermal + two adiabatic processes. Most efficient between T_H and T_L
questions.solution: η_carnot = 1 - T_L/T_H. All reversible. Used as standard for comparison.

questions.stem: On the tensile stress–strain curve shown for mild steel (yield ≈ 300 MPa), point A (end of the initial straight line) represents the:
questions.solution: Point A is the yield point where the linear elastic region ends; the peak (B) is the ultimate strength.

questions.stem: Water flows from a pipe at 211.0 kPa, 2 m/s at elevation 10m to ground level. Neglecting losses, the pressure (kPa) at ground level (velocity 24.9 m/s) is:
questions.solution: Bernoulli: p₂ = p₁ + ½ρ(v₁²−v₂²) + ρg(z₁−z₂) = -0.0000 kPa
